title: Review: simplify vault backend, document serde zeroization limit
description: Pre-merge review of commit 4781495: simplify vault backend and document serde zeroization limitation
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — the API change is clean and the symmetry is right, but the false zeroization claim ships to users who will make security decisions based on it, and the new code path has no tests.

Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) cannot inspect AWS SDK internals to confirm exactly how many String allocations the secret value passes through — I'm reasoning from how Smithy-generated AWS Rust SDK code works, not from reading its source; (2) cannot test whether Vault KV v2 actually accepts an empty data map (the put({}) edge case). Would raise confidence if: (a) grep the aws-sdk-ssm source for how GetParameter response is deserialized, confirming the zeroization characterization; (b) integration test run with VAULT_TOKEN present to exercise the new put-without-field path.
